tool_args.label: NYCT 2024 Budget and 2024-2027 Financial Plan Adoption Staff Summary

payload.title: NYCT 2024 Budget and 2024-2027 Financial Plan Adoption

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Expansion of Automated Bus Lane Enforcement program to be equipped with ABLE equipment and expenses to include event processing fees, fines processing fees, software, and contract management costs. Estimated revenues from fines collected are expected to offset exceed the operating expenses. Also aligned with operational efficiencies providing bus lane violation revenues and unscheduled bus operation overtime savings through camera installation on additional buses and expanded enforcement.

tool_args.label: Eagle Teams Fare Validation and Enforcement

payload.description: Expansion of Eagle Teams to provide fare validation and enforcement at various locations as recommended by the Fare Evasion Blue Ribbon Panel.
Maintenance of communications network infrastructure to support the implementation of OMNY, NYCT's new fare payment platform.
Replace, as part of the Faster, Cleaner, Safer strategic plan, fluorescent lighting with LED in stations and subway cars to increase customer satisfaction and reduce maintenance and energy expenses.

Outfit 1,188 Paratransit vehicles with an on-board vehicle surveillance camera system with real-time viewing and cloud video storage capabilities to promote safe vehicle operations and document accidents and customer satisfaction.
TA Committee meeting for NYCT 2024 Budget and 2024-2027 Financial Plan Adoption
Finance Committee meeting for NYCT 2024 Budget and 2024-2027 Financial Plan Adoption
MTA Board meeting for adoption of NYCT 2024 Budget and 2024-2027 Financial Plan

payload.raw_value_text: $304 million for NYCT annually beginning in 2024

tool_args.label: Employee availability savings $341M over Plan period

payload.scope: NYCT Division of Subways and Division of Buses

payload.description: Overtime assignments will be strategically managed, ensuring compliance with timekeeping rules and reducing overtime hours in targeted functions
Addressing critical station and infrastructure cleaning previously handled via more costly third-party contracts
Adjusted the car equipment maintenance approach
Obtaining efficiencies in rail material acquisition
Implementing energy efficient initiatives throughout stations, yards and on subway cars
Reorganizing terminal station car cleaning assignments
Lengthening crew tours for more efficient train operations staffing
Changes to bus maintenance schedules utilizing predictive maintenance tools
Improved analysis of vehicle condition and performance to allow for better maintenance planning

Bus lane violation revenues and unscheduled bus operation overtime savings related to the Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) measures through camera installation on additional buses and expanded enforcement
Paratransit trip-booking improvements utilizing self-service functionality will create savings and provide a better Paratransit customer experience

payload.raw_value_text: Total baseline full-time and full-time equivalent positions are 50,465
Total end-of-year positions are projected at 50,033

tool_args.label: NYCT projections exclude MTA consolidated below-the-line adjustments
tool_args.raw_text: The NYCT projections do not include MTA consolidated below-the-line adjustments, including the estimated impacts of projected fare increases, which are presented as part of MTA consolidated materials.

payload.claim_text: The NYCT projections do not include MTA consolidated below-the-line adjustments, including the estimated impacts of projected fare increases, which are presented as part of MTA consolidated materials.

payload.description: NYCT's Division of Subways is identified as a division within NYCT

tool_args.label: TA Committee action on NYCT budget adoption

payload.description: The TA Committee considered the NYCT 2024 Budget and 2024-2027 Financial Plan Adoption on 12/18/23 as part of the Board Action Order

tool_args.label: Finance Committee action on NYCT budget adoption

payload.description: The Finance Committee considered the NYCT 2024 Budget and 2024-2027 Financial Plan Adoption on 12/18/23 as part of the Board Action Order

tool_args.label: MTA Board action on NYCT budget adoption

payload.description: The MTA Board was recommended to adopt the 2023 November Forecast, 2024 Final Proposed Budget, and Four-Year Financial Plan for 2024-2027 on 12/20/23
It is recommended that the MTA Board adopt the budget and related authorization to draw down Transit Adjudication Bureau (TAB) receipts and interest
